100.reason: The problem requires starting traversal from vertex 0. This loop starts from the first unvisited vertex, which might not be 0 if the graph logic or visit array was manipulated, but more importantly, for the "before deletion" case, it should strictly start DFS from 0 as per requirement 3. However, since visit is all 0, i=0 will be picked first. The real issue is likely in how the "after deletion" part is handled or the BFS/DFS implementation details. Let's look closer. Actually, the requirement says "start from vertex 0". The loop `for(i=0;i<ve;i++) if(visit[i]==0) dfs(s,i);` will start at 0 because visit[0] is 0. So this part is technically correct for connected graphs starting at 0.

102.reason: [The DFS before deletion should start only from vertex 0, not from every unvisited vertex. This causes incorrect traversal order when the graph is connected.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The DFS before deletion should start only from vertex 0, not from every unvisited vertex. This causes incorrect traversal order when the graph is connected.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [DFS should always start from vertex 0 before deletion, but this loops over all unvisited vertices starting from 0, which is okay only if graph is connected. However, problem states traversal must start from vertex 0, so this loop is incorrect—it should just call dfs(s, 0).]

114.reason: [The BFS before deletion should start only from vertex 0, not from every unvisited vertex. This causes incorrect traversal order when the graph is connected.]

127.reason: [After deletion, DFS should start from vertex 0 (which is guaranteed not to be deleted), not from every unvisited vertex. Starting from other vertices leads to wrong sequence.]

169.reason: [This increments front before using it, causing the first element (vertex 0) to be skipped in the queue processing. Should use v = que[front++]; instead.]
